behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at the OS watcher boundary (recursive watch behavior). Required setup: run the real daemon, create a real nested subdirectory under a temp watched root, write a file, and verify processing/output. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher events can falsely pass even if recursive OS watching is misconfigured. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly depends on files "inside a subdirectory of the watched root" being observed by the live watcher, which disqualifies pure module-only tests.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against real temp input/output directories. After startup, create input/sub/a.txt with valid content. Assert exactly one transformed output is produced for that file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between request intake, dedup cache/window config, and pipeline dispatch. Required setup: wire daemon modules in-process (real dedup state + queue/dispatcher), inject two requests for same path inside window, assert one dispatch. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required for this behavior text (it says "processing request," not filesystem event semantics); (b) no AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: no concrete behavior text requiring real filesystem signaling.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate processing service with real dedup component and configurable window (e.g., 5s). Submit request(path=X), then submit request(path=X) again within 5s. Assert downstream transform is invoked once and second request is marked dropped.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error from real OS file event burst/coalescing behavior. Required setup: real filesystem + real watcher under daemon process; perform a write pattern that produces multiple events; verify single processing. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single-event or idealized mocks hide duplicate event patterns. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly says "operating system emits multiple filesystem events," which requires OS-level observation.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real temp directory. Write one file using a method known to trigger multiple events (create + append/rename). Capture processing records and assert exactly one pipeline execution for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error in watcher reattachment across real directory deletion/recreation (inode/path lifecycle). Required setup: live daemon + real directory removal/recreation while process stays up, then write a new file and verify processing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher callbacks cannot reproduce OS-level watch invalidation/rebind behavior reliably. Rejected lower level: behavior text requires handling a real directory being "removed and then recreated while the daemon is running."
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching temp input dir. Delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then create a new file. Assert daemon remains running and processes the new file without restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at boundary between real file metadata timestamps and queue ordering under near-simultaneous arrivals. Required setup: real filesystem timestamps and real daemon ingestion of multiple files created concurrently, then verify processing order. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps/events may pass while real filesystem timestamp resolution or event ordering breaks intended behavior. Rejected lower level: behavior text ties ordering to "creation timestamps" of arriving files, which is an OS-provided attribute in the running daemon context.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on real temp dirs. Create N files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times (or staggered by small known intervals), then assert observed processing sequence matches ascending creation timestamp policy.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error involving real write-in-progress timing and watcher/read coordination. Required setup: real daemon + writer that keeps file open/append in chunks while arrival event has already fired; verify processing starts only after completion condition. MOCK-HIDE: mocked "write complete" signals often hide race conditions around partial reads and file stabilization. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly depends on a file "still being written" at event time, a real OS/filesystem timing condition.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watched directory. Begin writing a large file in chunks with delays while keeping write stream open. Confirm no processing occurs mid-write; after stream close, assert single successful processing occurs.
